edges.description: This report will give you a high level overview of Assessments within a Course
This report will give you a high level overview of Assignments within a Course.
This report will give you a high level overview of Certificates within a Course.
This report will give you a high level overview of credits which are tied to an earned certificate. Users who have earned credits which are tied to an earned certificate are included in this report.
This report displays the total number of courses available. Each row contains detailed information about the available courses.
This report includes content and user activity for presentations included within courses across your site
This report will give you a high level overview of credits earned for items completed, regardless of whether the user earned a certificate
This report will give you a high level overview of data for all On-demand Events on your account. Each row contains content, visit, and status information for each event.
This report will give you a high level overview of all data pertaining to your in-person (or on-site) event objects in Path.
This report will give you a high level overview of web events on Path
This report contains a list of all SCORM content and general user engagement metrics, such as date first viewed, date completed, and item visits
This report will give you a high level overview of survey data within a course.
This report will give you a high level overview of user information regarding work completed over the given start/end dates inputted.

This query will return the list of all the coupons in the account that have not been deleted. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query will return the list of all the discounts in the account that have not been deleted. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150. The difference between this query and the coupons query is the data that is returned in the fields under this top level report.

This query will return the list of all the external activities in the account that have not been deleted, falls within the date range between the specified start and end date parameters. It will also take into account a list of permitted users based off of the permissions laid out in Path. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query will return a list of Orders in the account that have not been deleted, has been paid, was created within the date range between the specified start and end date parameters, or paid at between the specified start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150. It will also take into account a list of permitted users based off of the permissions laid out in Path.

This query will return a list of Order Items in the account that have not been deleted, has been paid, was created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query will return every sellable item in Path that has an item ID for an account. This item is sellable and has not been deleted. This page is commonly used to find the IDs of products for integration purposes. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query will return a list of Order Items in the account that have not been deleted, was refunded within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query will return a list of Orders in the account that have not been deleted, a Refund was created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query will groups sales, orders and revenue by the category of items included in the purchase transaction. This uses order and order items within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query groups orders, revenue, and credit balances by user. This report is useful for things like: understanding all-time revenue for individual users, viewing and exporting a list of all orders for an individual user or viewing credit usage within the range of the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query groups sales and revenue by order item (content purchased). This report will also include archived content if the withArchived parameter is set to true. It uses the order items that were created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query gets details for content purchased for an order item. It uses a course item type and id and gets orders that were created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

This query retrieves a list of Order Items. It filters the results based on a set of criteria, including the date range, user IDs, and product types. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
